summary: Small assembly data cleanup that replaced raw address literals in asm/sdata2.s with symbolic label references. The PR added global labels in asm/data.s at the corresponding data addresses lbl_803D9EE8, lbl_803D9EF4, and lbl_803D9F00, then used those labels for repeated .4byte pointer entries in sdata2. No discussion or review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Added .global symbols and labels for three data locations in asm/data.s: lbl_803D9EE8, lbl_803D9EF4, and lbl_803D9F00.
Changed sdata2 pointer words at lbl_804DA6B4 through lbl_804DA6D0 from numeric address constants such as 0x803D9EE8 to symbolic references such as lbl_803D9EE8.
Preserved the pointed-to data bytes and the pointer table contents; the change was representational rather than behavioral.
smart_moves: Converted repeated raw addresses into relocatable symbolic references, making pointer intent explicit and reducing dependence on magic constants.
Labeled the target data in asm/data.s before referencing it from sdata2, which keeps cross-section references understandable and assembler-resolvable.
Used address-derived temporary label names consistent with existing decomp style when semantic names were not available.
decomp_lessons: When a .4byte value in sdata2 is actually a pointer into another asm section, prefer a label reference over a raw hexadecimal address.
Before replacing raw pointer constants, ensure the target address has a label and is exported with .global if referenced across files or sections.
Even without semantic symbol names, address-based labels improve searchability and make future decompilation/refactoring safer.
Small data cleanup PRs can improve later C migration by distinguishing numeric constants from pointers.
naming_conventions: Temporary labels use the address-derived lbl_XXXXXXXX convention, e.g. lbl_803D9EE8.
Global asm labels are declared with .global immediately before the label definition when they need to be referenced elsewhere.
No semantic names were introduced; the PR stayed with mechanically generated address labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace .4byte 0xADDRESS with .4byte lbl_ADDRESS when the word is a pointer rather than a scalar constant.
Add labels at exact existing data positions without changing the emitted data words.
Use symbolic references to create proper relocation-style assembly intent while maintaining identical binary output.
follow_up_queries: Find other .4byte 0x803Dxxxx entries in sdata2 that may be pointers into asm/data.s.
Check whether lbl_803D9EE8, lbl_803D9EF4, and lbl_803D9F00 later received semantic names.
Search for remaining raw address literals in asm/sdata2.s that should be converted to label references.
